schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

address.streetAddress: G-131, Ground Floor, Phase 3, Spencer Plaza Mall, Anna Salai
Workafella, AK Estate, SV Road, off Veer Savarkar Flyover, Goregaon West

schemas.name: Credentials & Certifications | Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: Verified credentials: IBBI Registered Valuer IBBI/RV/03/2019/12333, ICAI Fellowship (FCA), ICSI Membership (ACS), ACFE Certification (CFE). Verification links and registration numbers.

alumniOf.name: The Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI)
The Institute of Company Secretaries of India (ICSI)

mainEntity.name: Why do credentials matter for valuation services?

acceptedAnswer.text: Under Indian law, only an IBBI Registered Valuer can issue valuation reports required under the Companies Act (Section 247) and IBC (Regulation 35). A generic CA certificate is not sufficient. Additionally, FEMA valuations require certification by a CA in practice, and SEBI-regulated valuations require independence from the transacting parties. Credentials determine legal validity of the work product.

mainEntity.name: Can I independently verify these credentials?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Every credential listed has a public verification mechanism: IBBI registration at ibbi.gov.in, ICAI membership at icai.org, ICSI membership at icsi.edu, and ACFE certification at acfe.com. Registration numbers are provided for verification.

mainEntity.name: Is the CFE credential recognised in India?

acceptedAnswer.text: The CFE is globally recognised but not mandated by any Indian regulatory framework. However, the ACFE methodology for fraud examination is the international standard used by law enforcement, regulatory agencies, and forensic practitioners worldwide. In India, CFE-qualified professionals are engaged by courts, SFIO, and corporate boards for fraud investigations.

Fellow of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI). FCA is awarded after 5+ years of practice as ACA (Associate CA). Demonstrates extensive experience in audit, tax, and financial advisory.

Associate of the Institute of Company Secretaries of India (ICSI). Qualifies for corporate governance, board compliance, ROC filings, and statutory secretarial work. Essential for Companies Act expertise.

Certified Fraud Examiner from the Association of Certified Fraud Examiners (ACFE), USA. The global standard for fraud examination. Qualifies for forensic investigations, due diligence, and anti-fraud advisory.

Registration with the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India under IBBI (Registered Valuers) Regulations, 2017. CA V. Viswanathan holds IBBI/RV/03/2019/12333 for Securities and Financial Assets class.

Each credential covers a different regulatory domain. FCA: financial analysis. ACS: corporate governance. CFE: fraud detection. IBBI RV: statutory valuation. The intersection enables multi-regulatory practice that single-credential firms cannot offer.
